_documentation.purpose: Branch-side fuel levy proposals register. Captures suppliers that have proposed, been modelled at, or are observed invoicing a fuel levy without a sighted Council-issued contract variation letter. Non-authoritative for payment.
_documentation.authority: This register is NOT authority to pay a fuel levy line. Payment authority comes only from fuel_components_register.json, which holds sighted contract variations. The proposals register exists so that the verifier can name the proposed rate in a BLOCK verdict, which helps the reviewer triage whether the invoiced levy is at least internally consistent with what the Branch register suggested.
_documentation.update_rule: Add a supplier here when an invoice arrives carrying a fuel levy line and the supplier is not in fuel_components_register.json. When a Council-issued contract variation is subsequently issued for the supplier, MOVE the entry to fuel_components_register.json (do not duplicate). Mark the proposals row with status 'SUPERSEDED_BY_VARIATION' for audit-trail purposes.
_documentation.source: Branch fuel levy proposals register, compiled 25-Mar-2026. Only suppliers presently observed invoicing without a sighted variation are added here; the full Branch register is referenced separately when needed.

suppliers.source_document: Branch fuel levy proposals register, Sara Pryor entry, 25-Mar-2026
suppliers.observed_behaviour: INV-1449 and INV-1450 dated 8-May-2026 invoice a fuel levy line. INV-1449 effective rate 7.023% (work $7,237.81, levy $508.28). INV-1450 effective rate 7.449% (work $1,897.48, levy $141.34). Same supplier, same work date 1-May-2026, different effective rates: structurally inconsistent. No contract variation sighted.
